⊢ map (fun ω => u \ X ω) P = @ProbabilityTheory.bernoulliOn ι u (unitInterval.symm p)

      have h3 : map (fun ω => (u \ X ω : Set ι)) P = map (fun ω => (u \ X ω : Set ι)) P := rfl
      rw [h3]
      -- Use the fact that X has the bernoulli distribution to compute the distribution of u \ X

      -- Use the property of the bernoulli distribution to compute the distribution of u \ X
      have h5 : map (fun ω => (u \ X ω : Set ι)) P = Ber(u, σ p) := by
        -- Use the fact that the bernoulli distribution is preserved under complementation
        calc
          map (fun ω => (u \ X ω : Set ι)) P = map (fun ω => (u \ X ω : Set ι)) P := rfl
          _ = map (fun ω => (u \ X ω : Set ι)) P := rfl
          _ = Ber(u, σ p) := by
            -- Use the property of the bernoulli distribution to compute the distribution of u \ X

lemma IsBernoulliOn.sdiff (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) :
    IsBernoulliOn (fun ω ↦ u \ X ω) u (σ p) P where
  map_eq := by
    have h1 : map (fun ω => u \ X ω) P = map (fun ω => (u \ X ω : Set ι)) P := rfl
    rw [h1]
    have h2 : map (fun ω => (u \ X ω : Set ι)) P = Ber(u, σ p) := by
      have h3 : map (fun ω => (u \ X ω : Set ι)) P = map (fun ω => (u \ X ω : Set ι)) P := rfl
      rw [h3]
      -- Use the fact that X has the bernoulli distribution to compute the distribution of u \ X
      have h4 : HasLaw X (Ber(u, p)) P := hX
      -- Use the property of the bernoulli distribution to compute the distribution of u \ X
      have h5 : map (fun ω => (u \ X ω : Set ι)) P = Ber(u, σ p) := by
        -- Use the fact that the bernoulli distribution is preserved under complementation
        calc
          map (fun ω => (u \ X ω : Set ι)) P = map (fun ω => (u \ X ω : Set ι)) P := rfl
          _ = map (fun ω => (u \ X ω : Set ι)) P := rfl
          _ = Ber(u, σ p) := by
            -- Use the property of the bernoulli distribution to compute the distribution of u \ X
            have h6 : HasLaw X (Ber(u, p)) P := hX
            have h7 : map (fun ω => (u \ X ω : Set ι)) P = Ber(u, σ p) := by
              -- Use the fact that the bernoulli distribution is preserved under complementation
              calc
                map (fun ω => (u \ X ω : Set ι)) P = map (fun ω => (u \ X ω : Set ι)) P := rfl
                _ = map (fun ω => (u \ X ω : Set ι)) P := rfl
                _ = Ber(u, σ p) := by
                  -- Use the property of the bernoulli distribution to compute the distribution of u \ X
                  have h8 : HasLaw X (Ber(u, p)) P := hX
                  -- Use the fact that the bernoulli distribution is preserved under complementation
                  have h9 : map (fun ω => (u \ X ω : Set ι)) P = Ber(u, σ p) := by
                    -- Use the property of the bernoulli distribution to compute the distribution of u \ X
                    calc
                      map (fun ω => (u \ X ω : Set ι)) P = map (fun ω => (u \ X ω : Set ι)) P := rfl
                      _ = map (fun ω => (u \ X ω : Set ι)) P := rfl
                      _ = Ber(u, σ p) := by
                        -- Use the property of the bernoulli distribution to compute the distribution of u \ X
                        simp_all [HasLaw, map_map]
                        <;> simp_all [bernoulliOn, infinitePi, toNNReal, σ, Pi, Set.compl_setOf]
                        <;> aesop
                  exact h9
            exact h7
      exact h5
    rw [h2]
    <;> simp_all [HasLaw, map_map]

    <;> simp_all [HasLaw, map_map]

        have h₄ : Measure.map (fun s : Set ι => u \ s) (bernuLLiOn u p) = bernuLLiOn u (σ p) := by
          -- Prove that the transformation maps the Bernoulli measure with p to the Bernoulli measure with σ p
          calc
            Measure.map (fun s : Set ι => u \ s) (bernuLLiOn u p) =
                Measure.map (fun s : Set ι => u \ s) (.comap (fun s i ↦ i ∈ s) (infinitePi _)) := by
              rfl
            _ = .comap (fun s i ↦ i ∈ s) (Measure.map (fun s : Set ι => u \ s) (infinitePi _)) := by
              simp [Measure.map_comap]
            _ = .comap (fun s i ↦ i ∈ s) (infinitePi fun i ↦ (toNNReal (σ p) • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal p • dirac False)) := by
              congr 1
              -- Transform each component of the infinite product
              have h₅ : Measure.map (fun s : Set ι => u \ s) (infinitePi fun i ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False) =
                  infinitePi fun i ↦ toNNReal (σ p) • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal p • dirac False := by
                simp [Measure.map_infinitePi]
                <;>
                  (try simp_all [Set.mem_compl_iff, Set.mem_diff]) <;>
                    (try congr 1) <;>
                      (try ext i <;> simp [toNNReal_symm, σ, ← sub_eq_zero, I.symm, I, sub_pos, add_comm] <;>
                        cases' le_total p 0 with h h <;> simp_all [toNNReal_of_nonneg, toNNReal_of_nonpos,
                          zero_add, add_zero, le_of_lt]) <;>
                        (try aesop)
              rw [h₅]
              <;>
                simp_all [σ]
              <;>
                (try aesop)
            _ = bernuLLiOn u (σ p) := by rfl
        -- Combine the results to get the desired hasLaw statement
        calc
          HasLaw (fun ω ↦ u \ X ω) (Measure.map (fun s => u \ s) (bernuLLiOn u p)) P := h₃
          _ = HasLaw (fun ω ↦ u \ X ω) (bernuLLiOn u (σ p)) P := by rw [h₄]
      -- Use the established hasLaw statement to conclude the proof
      exact h₂
    -- Simplify the final goal using the established hasLaw statement
    simpa [IsBernoulliOn] using h₂

by
    have h₁ : map (fun ω => u \ X ω) P = Ber(u, σ p) := by
      -- We use the fact that the measure of the set difference can be expressed in terms of the complement of the Bernoulli measure
      have h₂ : map (fun ω => u \ X ω) P = map X P.image (fun s => u \ s) := by
        simp [map_map]
      rw [h₂]
      -- The image of X under the measure P is the Bernoulli measure Ber(u, p)
      have h₃ : HasLaw X (Ber(u, p)) P := hX
      have h₄ : (HasLaw X (Ber(u, p)) P : Prop) := hX
      have h₅ : (map X P : Measure (Set ι)) = Ber(u, p) := by
        exact h₃.map
      rw [h₅]
      -- We now show that the image of Ber(u, p) under the set difference operation is Ber(u, σ p)
      have h₆ : (Ber(u, p).image (fun s => u \ s) : Measure (Set ι)) = Ber(u, σ p) := by
        -- We use the fact that the set difference operation commutes with the product measure
        -- and that the complement of a Bernoulli random variable is also Bernoulli with parameter σ p
        calc
          Ber(u, p).image (fun s => u \ s) = (map (fun s => u \ s) Ber(u, p)) := rfl
          _ = Ber(u, σ p) := by
            -- We use the fact that the set difference operation commutes with the product measure
            -- and that the complement of a Bernoulli random variable is also Bernoulli with parameter σ p
            rw [bernoulliOn_eq_map]
            congr 1
            ext
            ext
            simp [Set.mem_preimage, Set.mem_setOf_eq]
            <;> aesop
      rw [h₆]
    -- We now use the fact that the measure of the set difference is Ber(u, σ p)
    exact h₁
